data_schema: id(int); f_00..f_16 float; f_17..f_26 int; f_27 STRING (10-char code, engineer per-char features); f_28 float; f_29,f_30 int; target(0/1). Binary classification, metric ROC AUC. sample_submission: id,target (target = predicted probability in [0,1]).
extra_notes: Large data (900k rows). Keep compute modest: use LightGBM with reasonable params, 3-5 folds. f_27 is a 10-character string; a strong known trick is splitting it into per-position character features. CV_SCORE = mean OOF ROC AUC (higher is better). submission target column holds probabilities.
